description:
This image displays two panels, (a) and (b), showing Interferometric Synthetic Aperture Radar (InSAR) products.

Panel (a) presents an interferogram acquired between 01 August 1995 and 02 April 1996, with a baseline (Bn) of 44 meters and a temporal baseline (T) of 245 days. The image shows grayscale concentric fringe patterns, characteristic of land surface deformation, on a coordinate system with "North Direction - Step 125 [m]" ranging from 50 to 250, and "East Direction - Step 125 [m]" ranging from 50 to 350. A compass rose indicates North.

Panel (b) shows two grayscale satellite images side-by-side with an orientation arrow for North and labelled directions for "azimuth" (horizontal) and "range" (vertical). The left image is labelled "Amplitude", representing radar signal intensity and showing detailed surface features such as rivers and urban areas. The right image is labelled "Interferogram", displaying diffuse, cloud-like grayscale patterns which represent phase differences, potentially related to atmospheric effects or large-scale deformation. A "50 km" scale bar is provided above the Interferogram.
